text:
DOMINION MANDATE DAILY DEVOTIONAL

THURSDAY, MAY 14, 2026

THE MYSTERY OF FATHERHOOD

BIBLE READING: MATTHEW 23:9; 2 SAMUEL 7:14-15; PROVERBS 23:22,24; JOHN 6:57

“Moreover, we have had Earthly fathers who disciplined us, and we submitted and respected them [for training us]; shall we not much more willingly submit to the Father of [a] spirits, and live [by learning from His Discipline]? ”Hebrews 12:9 (AMP)


Have you ever looked at a family and wondered why the father’s role feels so heavy and powerful? It is not just about providing money or giving orders: There is a Hidden Secret behind Fatherhood that many people miss. That Secret is what we call a Mystery, something God placed deep in the Order of Life. A Mystery is not a problem; rather it is a Truth God wants us to discover. In the Old Testament, the Hebrew word for father is “Ab” or “Abba,” which sounds like a child calling “Daddy.” In the New Testament, the Greek word is “Pater.” But Fatherhood is more than just a name. According to Ephesians 3:14-15, a father is a SOURCE, a NOURISHER and a PROTECTOR. It is not a title you earn by age or by having children. It is a Sacred Role that the Lord God Almighty Himself established. The Lord Jesus showed us this when He spoke about His Father in John 14:9-11. A true father cares for his family just the same way God our Father cares for us.

Think about a building. Before you see the beautiful house, there is a foundation buried in the ground. That foundation is the father. God works through fathers to start good things that continue for many generations. He made a Covenant with the Patriarchs Abraham, Isaac and Jacob to pass down His Blessings from father to child (Gen. 17:7; Ex. 3:15). God loves continuity. He does not start something only for it to die. Consider a maize seed planted in the soil: The first thing that happens is that the seed becomes a root. That root is the father, hidden but strong. Then a shoot comes up; that shoot is the mother, visible and caring. Later, the shoot produces cobs of maize; those are the children. Without the root, there is no plant. Without the father, the family has no anchor. Fathers ground their families in Love, Wisdom and good traditions. They keep the family heritage alive so that blessings and teachings reach the next generation.

A father’s words are not ordinary sounds. They carry Power to change a child’s life. Imagine a farmer speaking to his land. The land listens and produces fruit. In the same way, a son listens to his father’s words and his future responds. Proverbs 4:1-4 says that when a son treasures his father’s instruction, his whole life improves. Hearing from a godly father is like hearing from God: His words are not just advice; they are life. A well-known example is King Solomon. His father, David, taught him to value Wisdom above gold. Solomon did not ask for riches or long life; he asked for Wisdom because his father had planted that hunger in his heart (Prov. 4:3-5). That decision made Solomon the wisest king who ever lived. A father’s words can lift a son higher than any man-made ladder could ever do.

Do not ever treat your father’s words as small or useless. Proverbs 18:21(NKJV) says: “Death and life are in the Power of the tongue.” A father’s tongue can shape destinies. Even after a father has passed away, his words remain alive. They become like road signs and light posts for the son. Just as your lungs need oxygen to stay alive, your spirit needs your father’s wise words to walk in the right direction. Never throw away what your father speaks over you. It is a treasure that keeps giving.

PRAYER:

Heavenly Father, we thank You for the Sacred Order of Fatherhood which You created for our good. O Lord, please give us a Deeper Understanding of this Mystery so that we may honour our fathers rightly and receive every Blessing You have hidden in Fatherhood in Jesus’ Mighty Name. Amen.
